options.name: /v
options.description: --version        Show the version of the tool.

options.name: /l
options.description: --lengthen       Make all words 30% longer, to ensure that there is room for translations.

options.name: --lengthen-char
options.description:
Specifies the following character to use to make words longer. This is
applied before other transformations, such as --accents, so may not be
the character that appears in the final pseudo-localized output.

options.name: /a
options.description: --accents        Add accents on all letters so that non-localized text can be spotted.

options.name: /b
options.description:
--brackets       Add brackets to show the start and end of each localized string.
This makes it possible to spot cut off strings.

options.name: /c
options.description: --culture        Use the following string as the culture code in the output file name(s).

options.name: /o
options.description: --overwrite      Overwrites the input file(s) with the pseudo-localized version.

options.name: /f
options.description:
--force          Suppresses the confirmation prompt for the --overwrite option.
The default options, if none are given, are: /l /a /b.
